input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

+  <PropertyGroup>

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-4223bda65de24d32be7d0c6b3556575b/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks && dotnet build -c Release --framework net10.0 -v q 2>&1 | head -5 && dotnet exec $(find ~/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet -name 'BenchmarkDotNet.dll' -path '*/net8.0/*' | head -1 2>/dev/null || echo "none") 2>&1 | head -3; grep -r 'Net10\|Net100' ~/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet/ --include='*.cs' 2>/dev/null | head -5; python3 -c "
import subprocess, re
r = subprocess.run(['grep', '-r', 'Net10', '-l'], capture_output=True, text=True, cwd='/root/.nuget/packages/benchmarkdotnet/')
print(r.stdout[:500])
print(r.stderr[:500])
" 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The BenchmarkDotNet version might not have `Net100` yet. Let me check what the correct moniker is. In BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.x, .NET 10 might be `Net10_0` or similar. Let me try a different approach - use the `[SimpleJob]` attribute with a custom runtime configuration, or just use the default job which runs on all target frameworks.

Actually, let me just use `BenchmarkDotNet.Jobs.RuntimeMoniker` and check what's available, or better yet, use `[TargetFrameworkJob]` or just rely on the multi-targeting.

data.reasoningText:
The issue is that the version 0.15.8 may be too old or the net10.0 TFM build doesn't include this moniker. The `strings` output showed "Net10_0" in the net8.0 version of the DLL but maybe not in the one resolved. Let me check which version of BDN is being resolved. Let me look at this more carefully.

Actually, the problem might be that the `net10.0` TFM build of the project resolves a different version of BDN. Let me check the resolved version. Or maybe the `0.14.*` version spec resolves 0.14.0 which doesn't have Net10_0.


The wildcard `0.14.*` only matches 0.14.x versions, and 0.14.0 likely doesn't include the Net10_0 moniker, so I should bump the version constraint to 0.15.8 instead.
